**NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER**
**FRIDAY, September 1st**
**_A Note From Jeanie_**
**Dear Derek,**
**We’re pleased to let you know that on September 30th, the New Hampshire Republican State Committee will be hosting the 2017 RED Summit. The summit will include Republican leaders, activists, and Republican State Committee members from across the state. At the summit, we’ll work together to map out a victory plan for 2018. We’ve got some great speakers lined up and the day promises to be inspiring and educational. **
****
**In the next week, we will provide additional information on the speakers and the program, so stay tuned and be sure to save the date, Saturday, September 30th for this day-long event at St. Anselm’s College.**

**Sincerely, **

**Jeanie**

**PHOTO OF THE WEEK**
Our Governor Chris Sununu making phone calls at WMUR's Phone Bank for Hurricane Harvey relief efforts this past week. The efforts by WMUR raised over $300,000 for the American Red Cross!
**REPUBLICAN SPOTLIGHT**
Thank you for all of your submissions! This week we must acknowledge all Granite Staters - Republicans, Democrats, and Independents - who stepped up to the plate and volunteered their time and efforts for Hurricane Harvey efforts. Every effort helps make a difference for those affected by this storm!

name: New Hampshire Republican Party
subject: Join the President for a National Day of Prayer
body:
Derek,

With all of the chaos in the world, it is important to pause and allow a moment of reflection. President Trump has declared tomorrow, September 3 a National Day of Prayer. Please join President Trump and me in praying for those recovering from Hurricane Harvey who need our prayers right now.

Please feel free to drop off canned goods and other essential items to the New Hampshire GOP at 10 Water Street in Concord, NH. We will be preparing and sending out care packages to Texas - whatever you can offer to se nd out, please get it to State Party HQ by Friday, September 8th.

Sincerely,

Jeanie Forrester

| Dear Peter, In response to the devastation in Texas and Louisiana and as a historic gesture of faith, President Trump issued a proclamation **declaring Sunday, September 3rd, a National Day of Prayer** for the victims of Hurricane Harvey and for the national response and recovery efforts. "From the beginning of our nation, Americans have joined together in prayer during times of great need to ask for God’s blessing and God’s guidance,” the proclamation states. It goes on to say, “As response and recovery efforts continue, and as Americans provide much-needed relief to the people of Texas and Louisiana, we are reminded of Scripture's promise that "God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble." My Faith Votes encourages people of all religious traditions to come together in unity on Sunday.
